critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Sports event promotion campaign, notification session mixing, and absence of log replay are the three confounds. The 6% aggregate lift cannot be attributed to the feed ranker. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Model design improvements may be genuine. But the aggregate metric under these conditions is not a clean measure of feed ranking quality. Controlled analysis needed. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Consistent identification of all three issues.

SYNTHESIS:
Strong consensus. Aggregate 6% lift is confounded. Empirical test needed to establish whether the new ranker genuinely improves home-feed engagement.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: Session watch-time restricted to home-feed-originated sessions in event-neutral weeks; counterfactual log replay with both rankers on fixed request sample
- Success criterion: Improvement is negligible on controlled segments — confirming promotion and notification effects explain the aggregate lift
- Failure criterion: New ranker shows consistent positive improvement on event-neutral home-feed sessions — confirming genuine ranking quality gain

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time on home-feed non-event sessions; log replay on fixed request sample
empirical_test.success_criterion: Improvement negligible on controlled home-feed non-event segments
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker shows consistent improvement on event-neutral home-feed log replay
